name: Coastal Settlement Interior
visual_tone: bright, airy, whitewashed coastal-village interior; pale stone and tile in place of warm wood, with a striped-linen accent instead of deep reds

biome_substitution.notes: Inside_A2 kind 16/18/23/24/31 (warm plank) -> kind 17/25/39/47 (pale stone/mosaic), per contact-sheet study.
Same substitution target; this style pack does not distinguish a separate paneling variant.
Inside_A5 tile 1/9 (warm plank) -> tile 20/40 (pale stone), keeping the same 'stairs-tile one row above floor' threshold construction.
Inside_B row ~168-175 (quilted/pillow beds) -> tile 177 (orange/white cabana stripe), the clearest single-tile coastal signifier found on this sheet.
Inside_B tiles 56-59/60-63 (deep red curtain) -> tiles 48-51 (tan/cream curtain), or omit in favor of pale_stone_threshold's own brightness if no suitable pale curtain tile is confirmed.

Inside_A5 tile 42 is a passable blue patterned floor accent confirmed in the WO-0061 composited render. It replaces the rug without changing gameplay geometry. The earlier tile-78 citation was rejected after rendering because tile 78 is yellow brick, not blue check.

provenance.notes: WO-0060 'coastal settlement' style pack. All tags are abstract vocabulary; no tile IDs appear here (contact-sheet kind/tile numbers cited above are provenance evidence, not bindings -- the actual verified TilePalette instance, including passability/layer/adjacency confirmation, is WO-0060 task 2, owned by the engine-adapter-side palette tooling, not authored in this reusable-library repository). Evidence trail: atlas-tools/mapgen/compiler/style_study/wo0060/temperate-and-coastal-interior-study.md and its contact sheets. This project's asset library (the 'Inside' RPG Maker MZ RTP-derived tileset family) has no purpose-built nautical/beach props (no net, shell, anchor, or driftwood tiles were found on Inside_B or Inside_C) -- this style pack's coastal identity is built entirely from color and material choice (whitewash, pale stone, blue-checkered tile, striped linen) already present on the sheet, not from thematic prop substitution. That is a real, disclosed limitation, not an oversight.
